source_text: KENNY, SIR EDWARD, KT. BACHEL. (Created 1870).—Born 1800; sat in legislative council of Nova Scotia for 26 years, during 12 of which he was president of that body; called to the Senate, 1867; receiver-general, July, 1867; president of privy council, Oct. 1869; administrator of the government of Nova Scotia for several months in 1870. Retired from the senate, 1877.

McCLOSKY, JAMES HUGH, M.R.C.S. and L.M., Edinburgh, L.S.A., London.—Late colonial surgeon, member of legislative council, and J.P. for Labuan; late surgeon, orphan asylum, &c., British Guiana; assistant colonial surgeon, Malacca, 1st August, 1877; colonial surgeon and medical officer, immigration department, &c., province Wellesley, 1879; coroner for the province, 1879-81; is a J.P. for the colony; medical officer, leper asylum, Pulo Jeeyah, Penang, 1884.

flagged_value.position: colonial surgeon and medical officer, immigration department, &c.

source_text: MOIR, R. W. D.—Acting assistant government agent, commissioner of requests, and police magistrate, at Mulaitivu, Ceylon, 1856; commissioner of requests at Calpentyn, 1861; assistant government agent at Mulaitivu, 1861; acting landing surveyor, Colombo, April, 1865; appointment confirmed, September, 1865; acting collector of customs, Galle, 1866; assistant government agent and district judge, &c., Trincomalee, 1870; acting government agent, eastern province, 1878; confirmed, 1879; grain commissioner, 1879; government agent and fiscal, N.W. province, 18th April, 1883.
TILTON, JOHN.—Entered the Canadian civil service, 1857; civil service commissioner, 1880; deputy minister of fisheries, 30th June, 1884.

VAN KOUGHNET, LAWRENCE.—4th class clerk, department of Indian affairs, Canada, Feb., 1861; 2nd class, Jan., 1866; 1st class, 1st July, 1873; chief clerk, 1st July, 1870; deputy of superintendent-general of Indian affairs, 1st July, 1880, with rank of deputy minister.
WALLINGTON, EDWARD WILLIAM, B.A.—Lt., 3rd bat. Oxfordshire Light Infantry; private secretary to governor, Fiji, 1883; to governor, N.S.W., 1885.

WAYLEN, A. B.—Of the medical and vaccine board, and surgeon to Perth Prison, Western Australia, 1875; served for sixteen years in the convict department, as medical officer of the Guildford convict depot and out-stations; acting as immigration agent and officer in charge of poor-house from August, 1872, until the end of the following year.
LYNCH, HON. PATRICK JOSEPH.—Formerly M.L.A., W. Aust.; mem. of senate, Comwth. of Aust.; min. for wks., Comwth. of Aust., 14th Nov., 1916.

BAUER, J. C.—Commenced his professional studies at the Engineering College of Munich in 1854, and received a practical training as machinist; was five years in the public works department in India, engaged on constructive designs and estimates for railways, bridges, steam, hydraulic, and other engineering works, and directing works in progress of construction; passed in Hindustani, Ordoo, and Devanagari; resigned in 1870 to return to Europe; entered the R.E. department, became a prize-holder for architectural designs in public competition, and is a member of the Institution of Civil Engineers of Germany; was selected for the appointment of colonial engineer at the Gambia, and is a justice of the peace.

COFFIN, WILLIAM FOSTER.—Called to the Bar of Lower Canada, 1835; appointed assistant civil secretary, Lower Canada, 1838, and in following year, police magistrate; again appointed assistant civil secretary for police purposes, 1840, and subsequently, in same year, commissioner of police for Lower Canada; appointed, 1842, joint sheriff of district of Montreal, which office he resigned in 1851, in consequence of a sudden reduction of salary by statute; ordnance land agent, 1856; commissioner of ordnance and admiralty lands, 1873; declined the lieutenant-governorship of Manitoba, 1872; holds the rank of lieut.-colonel in the Canadian Militia.

DALY, SIR DOMINICK, KNT. BACHEL. (Creat. 1856).—Was appointed secretary to the province of Lower Canada in 1827 (having previously held the office of assistant secretary in that province for two years), on the union of the provinces of Upper and Lower Canada in 1840; was appointed secretary of the province of Canada, which office he held until 1848; in 1849 was appointed a commissioner of inquiry into the claims, &c. &c., of the New and Waltham Forests, which he held until the close of the commission in 1850-1; appointed lieut.-governor of Tobago, 1851, transferred to the government of Prince Edward's Island in 1854, which he held until 1859; and appointed governor and commander-in-chief of South Australia, Nov. 1861.

GRATTAN, MAJOR H.—Gazetted ensign 80th regiment, on the 15th June, 1855; promoted lieutenant, 2nd Queen's Royals, in Feb. 1857; served with this regiment in South Africa, also throughout the campaign of 1860 in North China, for which he has a medal with two clasps for Taku Forts and Pekin; exchanged in Feb., 1863, as a captain to the 35th Royal Sussex, and with which regiment he served in India until its return to England in 1868, and for one year was attached to the Punjaub frontier force in command of the detachment then holding Fort Akulghur, at Dera Ismail Khan; promoted to a brevet majority on March 28, 1874, appointed in October, 1874, private secretary and aide-de-camp to Lieutenant-general Sir Arthur Purves Phayre, G.C.M.G., K.C.S.I., C.B., governor of Mauritius, and in 1878, received the thanks of both the secretaries of state for the colonies and war for services rendered.

HART, FREDERICK RALPH, F.R.G.S., F.R.A.S., &c.—Appointed, on the 1st October, 1873, clerk of the peace for the western district, county of St. Patrick (Cedros, La Brea, and Guapo), and clerk of the petty civil courts comprised in the above-mentioned places; is author of "India Bound to Indo-China," "London Bound," "Scrambles in Venezuela," "Notes on the Suez Canal," taken by him on the spot in February, 1872, and published in 1876, &c.; is a member of the geographical society of Paris. Appointed, on the 1st July, 1874, clerk of the peace for Savanna Grande, in the county of Victoria, also for Oropouche, La Brea, and Guapo (circuit), in the western district of the county of St. Patrick, and clerk of the petty civil courts comprised in the above-mentioned districts; is author of a map of Venezuela on the largest scale yet produced, which was drawn by him after his survey in that country in 1872, and now at the geographical society's rooms, Paris; is a French and Spanish interpreter; appointed clerk of the peace, and clerk of the complaint court for the town of San Fernando, 1st Oct., 1878.

JOSEPH, SIDNEY.—Appointed clerk in the office of the secretary of state for the colonies, July, 1843; was private secretary to Rt. Hon. Sir Jas. Stephen for seven years, and subsequently acted in the same capacity to Mr. Merivale for ten years; promoted assistant clerk, November, 1858; first class clerk, 30th September, 1872: Parliamentary clerk of the colonial office.

KENNY, KT. BACHEL. (Created 1870), HON. SIR EDWARD KENNY. — Born 1800; sat in legislative council of Nova Scotia for 26 years, during 12 of which he was president of that body; called to the Senate, 1867; receiver-general, July, 1867; president of privy council, Oct. 1869; administrator of the government of Nova Scotia for several months in 1870. Retired from the senate, 1877.

KEENS, JAMES HENRY, C.M.G. (1877). — Formerly president of the privy council; assistant justice of the court of common pleas; member of the legislative council, 1845; and a member of the executive committee, Tobago; was master in chancery from 1857 to 1858; police magistrate from 1858 to 1856; administered government of Tobago in 1856, 1857, 1874, and 1877; was president of the last legislative assembly.

LANGTON, JOHN, M.A.—Sat in Canadian parliament from 1851 to 1855, when appointed auditor of public accounts for province; appointed auditor of the dominion, 30th May, 1868; and subsequently, deputy of the minister of finance, and secretary of the treasury board; declined the finance ministry, 1862; has been vice-chancellor of the University of Toronto.

LAURIER, HON. WILFRID. — Born 20th Nov., 1841; educated at L'Assomption College; took degree of B.C.L. at McGill University; called to the Quebec bar, 1865; edited 'Le Defricheur' newspaper for some years; sat in the Quebec assembly for Drummond and Arthabasha from 1867 to 1874, when he was elected for same constituency to house of commons; now represents Quebec East in that body; sworn of the Canadian privy council and was minister of inland revenue, Oct., 1877, until the resignation of the Mackenzie administration, Oct., 1878.

MCLOSKY, JAMES HUGH, M.R.C.S. and L.M., Edinburgh, L.S.A., London.—Late colonial surgeon, member of legislative council, and J.P. for Labuan; late surgeon, orphan asylum, &c., British Guiana; appointed assistant colonial surgeon, Malacca, 1st August, 1877.

PRESGRAVE, D. C.—Acting police magistrate, Penang; chief clerk in the treasury, Penang, 1st July, 1849; appointed to the municipal department as secretary, April, 1851, acting magistrate of police and commissioner of the court of requests, Penang, 24th Oct., 1864; appointed deputy coroner, Feb., 1865; twice appointed acting magistrate and commissioner of the court of requests, Province Wellesley, in 1865; and again appointed acting police magistrate and commissioner, court of requests, Penang, 9th May, 1870; and duly admitted as an advocate in the supreme court of the Straits Settlements.

flagged_value.position: acting magistrate of police and commissioner of the court of requests
acting magistrate and commissioner of the court of requests
acting police magistrate and commissioner, court of requests

source_text: RUPERT'S LAND, 2nd BISHOP OF. Appointed 1865.—RIGHT REV. ROBERT MACHRAY, D.D.—Late dean and fellow of Sidney Sussex College, Cambridge, and vicar of Madingley; graduated in 1855, when he was 34th wrangler. The diocese of Rupert's Land is 37,000 square miles in extent; and has a population of 200,000 souls, and comprehends the whole of the Hudson's Bay Company's settlements (or Prince Rupert's Land); it was erected into a see in 1849, and endowed by a legacy from the late W. Leith, Esq.

STAWELL, KNT. BACHEL. (Creat. 1857.)—SIR WILLIAM FOSTER STAWELL.—Chief justice of Victoria, 1857; called to the Irish bar, 1839. ST. BERNARD, J. J.—Is a colonial revenue officer, Grenada; assistant clerk, colonial secretary's office, 1867; colonial revenue officer, 1871; acting chief clerk, colonial secretary's office, 1872; acted as postmaster during 1876; acted as chief clerk in the treasury, 1877; chief clerk, colonial secretary's office, 1878 to April, 1880; colonial revenue officer and registrar of births and deaths, Carriacou. April, 1880.

WILLISFORD, F. C.—Was appointed a writer to the Government of Ceylon, 1862; acting commissioner of requests and police magistrate of Upper and Lower Dumbara, 1862; commissioner of requests at Calpentyn, June, 1864; landing and tide-surveyor at Galle, May, 1856; commissioner of requests, Colombo, September, 1867; police magistrate, Colombo, June, 1868; acting collector of customs, Galle, July, 1871; confirmed in appointment, December, 1871.

WILLISFORD, F. C.—Was appointed a writer to the government of Ceylon, 1862; acting commissioner of requests and police magistrate of Upper and Lower, Doombera, 1862; acting court of requests, Avishawalle, 1863; commissioner of requests at Calpentyn, June, 1864; landing and tide-surveyor at Galle, May, 1865; and acting collector of customs, southern province, July, 1865, acting landing surveyor, Colombo, June, 1866.
AMPUTCH, J., M.B.E. (1959)—b. 1911 ; labr. inspr., Fiji, 1944 ; labr. offr., 1950 ; senr., 1957 ; comsnr., labr., 1960.
COREE, J. P.—b. 1921; ed. St. Michael's Coll., Limerick, Nat. Univ. of Ireland, and Purdue Univ., U.S.A.; exec. engr., S. Leone, 1947; senr., 1954; asst. D.P.W., Aden, 1958; state engr., 1963.
COWLEY, A. G.—b. 1916; ed. Oswestry Gram. Sch.; asst. loco. supt., Pal. rly., 1946; E.A.R. and H., 1948; dist. mech. engnr., 1950; works man., 1955; asst. ch. mech. engnr., 1961–62.
ETIENNE, J. T., C.P.M.—b. 1906; ed. Royal Coll., Maur.; police constable, Maur., 1932; 1st cl. sgt., 1944; asst. supt. 1947; supt., police, 1952.
EWING, P. M., M.B.E. (1961).—b. 1900; ed. Baptist Day Sch., priv. tuition; copyist, audit dept., B. Hond., 1919; 3rd cl. clk., 1939; examr., accts., audit, 1947; asst. postmaster, 1951; P.M.G., 1954.

FAIRLEY, R. D.—b. 1922; ed. Cheltenham Coll.; mil. serv., 1940-46, R.A.F. (desps.); admin. offr., N. Heb., 1947; asst. sec., W. Pac. H.C., 1949; secon., spec. asst. to sec.-gen., S. Pac. Comsn., New Caledonia, 1951-52; dist. comsnr., B. Sol. Is. Prot., 1955; admin. offr., Nyasa., 1957-59.

HUGHES, O. G., O.B.E.—b. 1912; ed. Grey St. Sch., Bootle; supt., prisons, G.C., 1950; senr. supt., 1952; prin. supt., 1954; dep. dir., 1956; supt. prisons, Nig. 1958; senr. supt., 1961; dir. of prisons, S.L., 1964. (S.L. Govt. service.)

HUMPHREY, R. P.—b. 1910 ; ed. Belmont Coll., London ; cost acctnt., Br. P.O., 1939 ; senr., 1940 ; S.O.II (finance), P. and T. branch, control comsn., Germany, 1945 ; dep. contrl. (finance), 1946 ; fed. chief acctnt., Libya, 1952 ; asst. P.M.G. (finance) and chief acctnt., E.A.P. and T., 1956.

KENDALL, C. L.—b. 1913; ed. Pamphylian High Sch., Trin.; 4th cl. clk., Trin., 1934; 2nd cl., 1938; 1st cl., 1943; senr. clk., acctnt.-gen.'s dept., 1947; principal offr., 1950; acctnt., 1952; asst. sec., finance dept., 1956. (T'dad Govt. service.)

LEESTON-SMITH, K. S.—b. 1923; ed. Earleywood Sch., Berks., Stowe Sch., and Coll. of Aero. Engineering, Wimbledon; police cadet, Pal., 1947; asst. supt., Sarawak constab., 1948; dep. supt., 1956; supt., 1957; chief police offr., Brunei, 1959; asst. comsnr., constab., S'wak, 1960–63.
MALCOLM-SMITH, I.—b. 1929; ed. Prince of Wales Sch., Nairobi; junr. exam. offr., E.A.H.C., 1949; exam. offr., 1951; collr., customs, E.A.H.C. (E.A.C.S.O.), 1957; prin. collr., 1963.

NICHOLS, E. H.—b. 1925; ed. Royal Gram. Sch., Newcastle-on-Tyne, King's Coll., Durham Univ., Queen's Coll., Camb., and I.C.T.A.; agric. offr., S. Leone, 1947; senr., 1954; prin. agric. offr., 1957; asst. dir., agric., fish. and forestry, H.K., 1959.
PHELPS, G. F.—b. 1913; ed. Willaston, and Gonville and Caius Coll., Camb.; mil. serv., 1939–46, capt.; educ. offr., Tang., 1947; senr. educ. offr., 1958. (Tanzania Govt. service.)

POLLARD, G. F., O.B.E. (1964).—b. 1920; ed. Manchester Gram. Sch. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; mil. serv., 1939–46; admin. offr., Som., 1947; Nyasa., 1950; senr. gr. II, 10; gr. I, 1962–65; retd., apptd. temp. prin., C.O., 1965.

RAIT, D. M.—b. 1900; ed. Framlingham Coll., Suffolk, and City and Guilds (Engnrg.) Coll., S. Kensington; chief engnr., P.W.D., J'ca, 1946 (also govt. electrical inspr., 1952–56); chief electrical inspr., min. of Lagos affrs., mines and power, Fed. of Nig., 1956. (Nig. Govt. service.)
RAMPERSAD, I. C. F.—b. 1923; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Trin.; 2nd cl. clk., Trin., 1943; 1st cl., 1949; senr. clk., 1951; principal offr., 1954; asst. sec., 1956. (T'dad Govt. service.)

SHANMUGARATNAM, K.—b. 1921; ed. Methodist Boys' Sch., K. Lumpur; King Edward VII Coll. of Medicine, S'pore, and London Univ.; asst. pathol., S'pore, 1948; secon., lect., pathol., Univ. of Mal., 1949–51; senr. registr., pathol., 1956; pathol., 1958.

SKELT, G. J.—b. 1918; ed. Westcliff High Sch. and City and Guilds Coll., London Univ.; exec. engnr., P.W.D., S. Leone, 1947; senr., 1952; asst. D.P.W., Som., 1956; S. Leone, 1957; ch. engr., P.W.D., H.K., 1961; asst. govt. civil engnr., 1964.

STYLIANAKIS, N., M.B.E.—b. 1902; ed. English Sch., Nicosia; student clk., Cyp., 1917; 3rd div. clk., col. sec's office, 1927; Greek interpreter, leg. co., 1927–30; asst. registr., sup. court, 1930; registr. and interpreter, dist. court, 1930; asst. sec., 1944; admin. sec., medical dept., 1946; chief registr., sup. court, 1947.

TIBO, A. A.—b. 1916; ed. Govt. Boys' Sch. and Mfantsipim Sch., G.C.; police constable, G.C., 1938; sgt., 1944; sub-inspr., 1944; inspr., gr. II, 1948; asst. supt., 1950; supt., 1957; senr. supt., 1958). (Ghana civil service.)
VASSILOPOULOS, V. P.—b. 1916; ed. Univ. of Athens; mil. serv., 1942-46, capt., R.A.M.C.; M.O., cl. III, Cyp., 1946; cl. II, 1952; D.M.O., 1954; asst. D.M.S., 1958.
YORKE, F. G.—b. 1914 ; ed. St. Mary's Coll., St. L. ; labr. inspr., St. L., 1952 ; prin. clk., admin., 1955 ; supt., prisons, staff offr., spec. reserve, police, 1958.
CAULFIELD, FRANCIS ST. GEORGE, M.I.C.E.—Educated Trin. Coll., Dub.; superintendent, public works, surveyor and registrar of mines, Perak, Aug., 1871; state engineer and surveyor, Jan., 1884.

GUDEMANN, ROBERT.—For some time asst. acct. treas., Jamaica; clerk in audit office, Victoria, Mar., 1853; acct. to treas. and established new system of accts., Jan., 1856; services dispensed with on "Black Wednesday," Jan., 1878; inspector of the banks' clearing house in Feb., and recalled to office in the treas. in June; deputy commssnr. of audit, Mar., 1886; under treasurer, Feb., 1887.

TESSIER, ULRIC JOSEPH, I.L.D. — Justice, Court of Queen's Bench and Appeal Province Quebec, 1873; he was mayor of his native city, Quebec, in 1851; sat in the Canadian Parliament for the county of Portneuf, 1850-53; afterwards in the legislative council, 1859-66; was a member of the provincial cabinet in 1861 and 1862; president of the legislative council from 1859 to 1866, when the confederation was proclaimed; was then appointed one of the senators of the Dominion, which position he held until he was named a judge of the superior court; he has been since 1871 dean of the law faculty of the Laval university.

PINE, SIR BENJAMIN CHILLEY CAMPBELL PINE. KNT. BACHEL. (Creat. 1856), K.C.M.G. (1871).—Educated at Trin. Coll., Camb., B.A. 1834, M.A. 1840; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1841; Queen's advocate at Sierra Leone, 1841; acting governor there, 1848; accompanied Capt. Monypenny, R.N., to the Sherbro river, and destroyed a strong stockaded fort, from which outrages had been committed on British trading vessels. In 1849 again proceeded to Sherbro, with a detachment of troops, and put an end to the civil war which had ravaged the country for years; lieut.-governor of Natal, 1849; 1851 received the thanks of her majesty's government for the measures he took to preserve peace in the colony during the Kaffir war; in 1855, led a force of mounted volunteers against the Amabaca tribe, numbering 2,000 men, and completely enforced their submission; governor and commander-in-chief Gold Coast, 1866; lieut.-governor of St. Kitts, May, 1859; is author of some articles in the Encyclopedia Britannica on the "African Colonies;" acting governor, Antigua, 1866; governor-in-chief, Leeward Islands, Feb., 1869; created K.C.M.G. for his services in connection with the confederation of the Leeward Islands; lieut.-governor of Natal, 1878, until 1875; when he retired on pension.

BOTT, ARTHUR MOORE.—Clerk to attorney-general, British Guiana, 1871; clerk, colonial registry office, July, 1874; 3rd clerk, immigration department, Oct., 1876; stipendiary magistrate's Mar., 1879; compiler of Summary Conviction Ordinances of the Colony, 1837 to 1883; chief police magistrate and sheriff of Demerara; has several times acted as special and auxiliary J.P., ag. asst. pol. mag., Georgetown, 1890, to Jan., 1891, and May, 1893, to Feb., 1891; ag. stip. mag. Essequibo Islands, April, 1891, to 1892.
TCHLEY, C.—Entered the colonial office. g. 1868; served in chief clerk's, accountant's and eral departments; assistant clerk, second class, 5; supt. of the Library, 1st Oct., 1880.

OOTH, ROBT. MALCOLM.—Cadet, Fiji, Nov. 3; attached to col. sec. off., Feb., 1884; ditto, mag. off., Tai Levu, July, 1884; at Navua, v., 1884; at y.-gen.'s off., June, 1885 to Oct. 8; passed cadet, 1887; stip. mag., Tai Levu, 8; ditto. Lomai diti, 1893; ditto, Savu Savu I Bua, 1893; confirmed as stip. mag. and comm. sup. ct., 1894.

DOWNE, ALBERT.—Appointed, after a competitive examination, to be a clerk of the lower (now land) division of the civil service, and assigned to Colonial Office, 4th Sept., 1877, to 20th Oct., 1880; clerk in chief secretary's office, Cyprus, 21st Nov., 1880; returned to duty in Colonial Office, Jan., 1883; appointed to act for three years as assistant to the high commissioner for South Africa, April, 1891; acts also as auditor, Basutoland, from Oct., 1892; member of board of enquiry into postal and telegraph administration, Transvaal, April, 1892; ag. Imperial secy., to Mar., 1895; and (jointly with Capt.kins), Mar. to Aug., 1896; asst. to Imperial v. and accountant, Aug., 1895.

CADDY, DUNCAN JAMES, M.D., F.R.G.S., M.B.—Govt. medl. offr., Carriacon, 1892; ed., Merchant Taylors' and King's Coll, Lond. and Dub. Univ.; member Pathol. Soc. Lond., British Med. Assocn., and other learned societies; has contributed papers on medical subjects to the Lancet, Br. Med. Journal and other journals. CADE. CHARLES SHERWOOD.—Captain in the West Cork Artillery Militia; served through the Zulu War. 1878-9; present at the battle of Ulundi; assistant-inspector, Gold Coast Constabulary, Mar., 1880; inspector, 11th Dec., 1882; adjutant and musketry instructor of the Gold Coast Constabulary, Sept., 1882, to May, 1883; local commandant of police, Cyprus, 29th Sept., 1883; also assistant to commissioner, Nicosia, and governor, central prison, Oct., 1884; has acted several times as commissioner.

flagged_value.position: adjutant and musketry instructor of the Gold Coast Constabulary

source_text: LARK, ANDREW INGLIS.—Attorney-general of mania in the Fysh government, 29th Mar., 1892; member of the federal council of Austral., Jan., 1888; and of the federation convention, 1.

APPER, ALFRED HOUSTON. — Ed. Royal col. Armagh, and Trin. Coll., Dub., scholar- in classics; B.A. in 1883; cadet, S. S., Nov., l; J.P. and magistrate, May, 1884; passed l examination in Chinese, and attached to best councillor's office, Penang, 1886; 2nd st. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, 1888; district sr. 1889; ag. sen. dist. off., Butterworth, P. W., , 1889; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., April, 1890; protector of Chinese, Singapore, 1893; ag.. Mag. Penang, Sep. 1893; ag. 2nd asst. col. Sept., 1894; ag. asst. col. sec., Nov., 1894, to fl. 1895; asst. protr. of Chinese, Penang, April, l.

ARLILE, EDWARD. gold medallist in law, Melbourne Univ., 1868. — Clerk, census office, Victoria, 20th May, 1861; registrar general's be. 1st Jan., 1862; crown law offices, 1st Mar., 65; called to the bar, Victoria, 1871; parliament- and professional assistant to crown law officers 73; parliamentary draftsman, July, 1879; clerk- assistant, legislative assembly, April, 1882; re- pointed parliamentary draftsman with higher ans, April, 1889; author of "Notes on Victoria Institution," "Victorian Year Book, 1883-4."

flagged_value.position: parliament- and professional assistant to crown law officers

source_text: DOWNE, GEORGE.—Associate to the Judges of the Supreme Court of Tasmania, registrar of supreme court in bankruptcy, curator of intestate estates, and marshal of the vice-admiralty court, 1866; was clerk of the peace, deputy sheriff, registrar of the court of requests and of insolvency, Launceston, 1863; recorder of titles, acting registrar, supreme court, and collector of probate duties, 1875; was private secretary to administrators of the government, June, 1874-75; was acting state secretary to Governor Weld, 1876, 1877-79; was appointed private secretary, 1880; private secretary to governor, Straits, 1881, to Mar., 1884; to the administrator of Tasmania, April, 1884; acting private secretary to Sir G. C. Strahan, governor Tasmania, July, 1886, and to administrator Sir W. L. Dobson, 1886-7.

LARE, HENRY LEWIS.—Ed. Rathmines and Trin. coll., Dub.; B.A. (1879), M.B., and Ch. (1880); junr. resdt. med. offr. pub. hosp. Kingston, Jamaica, Apl., 1881; dist. med. offr. River dist. and hosp., July, 1882; transferred ere dist., 1883; to Chapleton, 1888; to Spanish n., 1892; surg. to St. Catherine dist. prison to Middlesex and Surrey cty. gaol; dist. med. Kingston, June, 1896.

ORD, RICHARD.—Ed. at Collegiate, Shaw St., Liverpool; served for three years with a firm of stockbrokers in that town; emigrated to Australia, 1852, and employed for 2 years in aitor's office in Melbourne; returned to England, 1854, and employed for 3 yrs. by the Liverpool Trust; returned to Australia in 1858, and 3 yrs. in a solicitor's office in Ballarat, commenced business as a sharebroker at Daylesford, subsequently as an accountant, auditor and managing agent at Ballarat; elected auditor to the council and town council there by a large majority, and afterwards chosen as town clerk and treasurer successively; after six years' service, on creation of the Melbourne harbour trust, he was chosen as secy., and received numerous testimonials and valuable testimonials on leaving Ballarat, as delegate on behalf of the trust successfully floated loan of 250,000l. in London in 1883, soon after his return appointed one of the managers of the line, and received testimonials from members of the trust, their officers, and the press, on his transfer; resigned, 1892.

UFFY, THE HON. SIR CHARLES GAVAN, M.G. (1877), (Kt. BACH. 1873).—Was called to Irish bar in 1845, but was early engrossed duties as editor of the "Nation" newspaper, one of the leaders of the national party known Young Ireland. Was a state prisoner along with O'Connell in 1844, and Smith-O'Brien in 1848. Member for New Ross in 1852, defeating Thomas Redington, chief secretary for Ireland. Resigned his seat and emigrated to Australia at the commencement of 1856, and practised for a short time at the bar in Melbourne. Was elected a member of the first parliament of Victoria, and in 1857 became minister of public works in the first responsible government. In 1858 became president of the board of land and works. In 1861 was recalled to the home office. In 1867, after two years' absence in Europe, was re-elected to Parliament for the first time, and shortly afterwards was appointed chairman of a royal commission to devise the best means for effecting a federation of the Australian colonies. In 1871 became prime minister; and in the same year was chairman of an intercolonial conference of Australian cabinet ministers to urge on the colonial office the removal of restrictions on colonial legislation, which change has since been effected. In 1876, after two years' absence in Europe, was again re-elected to Parliament by one of the largest constituencies in the colony without personally visiting the district, and on the assembly being dissolved in May, 1877, was unanimously elected to the office of speaker. After three years' service he resigned, in order to return to Europe. Has since published "Young Ireland—a Fragment of History," "Four Years of Irish History," and "The League of North and South."

AHN, D. M., A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Realschool, Hamburg; served apprenticeship under J. A. Ratzki, architect, Altona; theoretically instructed at the Technological Institution, Hamburg; in charge govt. saw mill, Trinidad, Jan., 1877; foreman, public works, Jan., 1888; ag. st. engineer, N. Div., in 1889, 1890, and 1891; st. engineer, N. Div., 1891.

RIXSON, FRANCIS, late Master, R.N.—Served fifteen years on Australian station, principally in surveying and exploring service; superintendent of pilots, lighthouses, and harbours, N.S. Wales, Jan., 1863; organised N. S. W. Naval Bde., 1863, and still commands it; President, marine board, April, 1872.

flagged_value.position: superintendent of pilots, lighthouses, and harbours
organised N. S. W. Naval Bde.

source_text: UDGE, EDWARD ARTHUR.—Magistrate's clerk, on's Town, May, 1856; clerk in Colonial Office, etown, Aug., 1857; acting C.C. and R.M., aqualand, 1861; C.C. and R.M., Namaqua- , 1864; C.C. and R.M., Queenstown, Dec., 6; inspector-general of chests, &c., 1875; act- C.C. and R.M., Capetown, and R.M., Wynberg. , 1877; C.C. and R.M., Worcester, Nov., 1877; and register of deeds, Kimberley, May, 1882; eld in the following commissions: boundaries ordrecht and settlement of Tambookie loca- , 1870; disturbances in Transkei, 1872; railway rs, Port Elizabeth, 1878; Northern Border dis- ances, 1879; Basutoland and Transkei war nditure, 1881.
GREGOR, JAMES COMYN.—2nd lieut. 7th m. K.R.R., 1881; lieut., 1881; sub.-inspector mtd. police, 1884; ag. asst. comsnr., 1; confirmed, 1893.

O'RORE, THE HON. SIR GEORGE MATTHEW KNIGHT BACHELOR (1880).—Educated at Trin. College, Dublin, of which he is a graduate, emigrated to Victoria in 1852, and settled in New Zealand in 1854; elected to the House of Representatives in 1861, for the Town of Onehunga, and sat continuously for that until it was merged in the elective district of Manukau, for which he has been since 1882; elected speaker of the House of Representatives, July, 1879; and a dissolution taking place in Aug., was re-elected speaker in Sept. following; on both occasions was elected unanimously; previous to this he had been elected chairman of committees of the House of Representatives, viz., in 1871, 1875, and 1877; has been also speaker of the Auckland provincial council for twelve years, from 1865 to the abolition of the province of Auckland in 1876; entered the Waterhouse ministry in 1872, as secretary of crown lands, and minister for immigration Waterhouse resigning the premiership in 73, he continued in the ministry of Sir Julius Wel until 1874, when he resigned; is a member of the New Zealand bar, and in 1879-80 was chairman of the royal commission to inquire into the conditions of the university of New Zealand, and secondary schools of the colony in their relation to the university; was for the 3rd and 4th time unanimously elected speaker of the House of Representatives. May, 1882, and Aug., 1884, respectively; again in 1887; was elected as first chairman of the council of the Auckland University College founded in 1883, and still holds that office; he is also chairman of the board of governors of the Auckland grammar school.

flagged_value.position: continued in the ministry of Sir Julius Wel

source_text: LELAND, LIONEL THOMAS HILLIER. — Capt., 4th Batt. S. Staffs. Regt. (late lieut., Worcester Regt.); ed. at St. Columba's Coll. and Dublin Univ.; 2nd lieut., Mid-Ulster Art., 1900; P.S. certif.; apptd. to take draft of Irish Yeomanry to S. Africa; attached to 1st. M.I.; operations, 1901-1902 (Queen's medal with five clasps); comsn., R.G.R., passed A. and B.; M.I. certif.; long course Hythe certif.; special topographical employment; served in Canada 3½ yrs., until withdrawal of Imperial garrison in 1905; transfd. to Worcester regt. and posted to 1st Batt., 1905; passed C. and D.; asst. instr. in topography; resigned, Nov., 1908; apptd. 2nd clk. to gov., Windward Is., Dec., 1908; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to Sir. J. H. Sadler, gov. of Windward Is., Aug., 1909; asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Mar., 1912.

flagged_value.position: transferred to Worcester regiment and posted to 1st battalion
priv. secretary and A.D.C. to Sir. J. H. Sadler, governor of Windward Is.
